v1.0.0.description:
First full release — the whole Darm Tower randomized, playable as **Yunica, Hugo, or Toal**, with real progression logic and an in-game client.

## Requirements
- **Ys Origin** on Steam — version **1.1.1.0** (current Steam build)
- **[Archipelago](https://archipelago.gg)** (to generate / host, or to join a host)

## Files in this release
- **`ys_origin.apworld`** — drop into Archipelago's `custom_worlds/` folder
- **`dinput8.dll`** — the in-game mod/client; drop into your Ys Origin game folder (next to `yso_win.exe`)
- **`Ys-Origin.yaml`** — settings template; edit your name + character, put it in Archipelago's `Players/`

## Quick start
1. Put `ys_origin.apworld` in `custom_worlds/`.
2. Edit `Ys-Origin.yaml` (name + character: yunica/hugo/toal), put it in `Players/`.
3. Archipelago Launcher → **Generate** → **Host**.
4. Copy `dinput8.dll` into `...\steamapps\common\Ys Origin\`.
5. Launch the game once, then edit the `yso_ap.cfg` it creates (host / port / slot).
6. Launch, load a save, play. Overlay is top-right; toggle with **INSERT**.

Full step-by-step + troubleshooting in the [README](../../blob/master/README.md).

## What's randomized
Chests, key-item events, goddess statues, and divine blessings (~155 checks), with optional boss / floor / room sanity checks. The generator models the tower's gates (wind, dash, water-breathing, thunder, fire, climb, the keys, and the boss medallions), so **every seed is completable**. Each character receives their own equipment variants. Goal: defeat Darm (or all bosses).
v1.0.0.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.0

v1.1.0.description:
Adds **DeathLink** on top of 1.0.

## What's new
- **DeathLink** (opt-in): when enabled, you die if any other DeathLink player dies, and your deaths are sent to them. Off by default — set `death_link: true` in your yaml. The mod detects your death and forces one via the game's real combat HP (clean natural Game Over, no crash).

## Files
- `ys_origin.apworld` → Archipelago `custom_worlds/`
- `dinput8.dll` → your Ys Origin game folder (next to `yso_win.exe`)
- `Ys-Origin.yaml` → settings template for `Players/` (now includes `death_link`)

## Requirements
- Ys Origin (Steam) **v1.1.1.0**
- [Archipelago](https://archipelago.gg)

Setup is unchanged from 1.0 — see the [README](../../blob/master/README.md). To use DeathLink, set `death_link: true` and host with DeathLink enabled.
v1.1.0.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.1 — DeathLink

v1.2.0.description:
## New: optional Goddess Statue Warp Locks

Turn the goddess statues into a warp network. With **`statue_warp_locks: true`**:

- Every statue starts **locked** — wrapped in darkness, with no warp, healing, or saving — until you receive its **Statue Warp** unlock item (21 new items shuffled into the multiworld).
- **One statue is unlocked from the start** so you can always save (the 1F starting statue by default, or a random one with **`random_start: true`**).
- Receiving a statue's unlock item makes it **immediately warpable and usable** (no need to revisit it).
- Statue location checks still fire normally, and every seed stays completable on foot — the unlocks are pure convenience.

Off by default; existing options are unchanged.

### Assets
- `ys_origin.apworld` — drop in Archipelago's `custom_worlds/` (generation).
- `dinput8.dll` — the in-game mod/client; drop next to `yso_win.exe`.
- `Ys-Origin.yaml` — player config template (now documents the new options).

See the README for the full setup and options table.
v1.2.0.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.2 — Statue Warp Locks

v1.3.0.description:
Makes the goddess-statue **warp network** actually playable when it drops you far above your level — by scaling **both** halves of combat: your level *and* your weapon.

## What's new

- **Weapon gating + auto-upgrade.** Your weapon is the dominant damage stat in Ys Origin. Cleria Ore is now shuffled into the pool as **progression** and, when received, **upgrades your weapon directly** (no NPC trip). With `weapon_requirements` on (default), the generator guarantees the **vanilla weapon level for each floor** is obtainable before that zone is in logic — so a warp ahead can't strand you somewhere your weapon deals 1 damage.
- **Catch-up level scaling.** `level_scaling` (default `both`): a **level floor** bumps an under-leveled character to the floor's expected level, and/or an **EXP multiplier** scales with how far under you are (tapering to 1× on level). Tune with `level_margin` (default `0` = land right at the expected level) and `exp_multiplier_max`.
- Floor→level curve recalibrated to the real vanilla progression (21F Mantid ≈ Lv41, 25F ≈ Lv51).

## New options

| Option | Values | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `level_scaling` | off / level_floor / exp_multiplier / both | both |
| `level_margin` | 0–10 | 0 |
| `exp_multiplier_max` | 1–20 | 8 |
| `weapon_requirements` | true / false | true |

## Install

1. `ys_origin.apworld` → Archipelago's `custom_worlds/`.
2. `dinput8.dll` (the mod) → your Ys Origin folder (next to `yso_win.exe`).
3. Edit `Ys-Origin.yaml` → `Players/`, generate + host, set `yso_ap.cfg` to the room. `INSERT` toggles the overlay.
v1.3.0.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.3 — Weapon Upgrades & Level Scaling

v1.3.1.description:
The mod now connects **directly to archipelago.gg over `wss://` (TLS)** — the local ws→wss bridge is no longer needed.

## What's new
- **Native `wss://` support.** Set `host=wss://archipelago.gg` (with the room's port) in `yso_ap.cfg` and the mod connects straight to the room over TLS. CA certs are read from the Windows root store; nothing extra to install. Plain `ws://` still works for local hosting.
- The mod (`dinput8.dll`) is now ~4.5 MB — OpenSSL is **statically baked in**, so it's still a single self-contained DLL (no extra files to ship).

Includes everything from 1.3 (weapon-upgrade gating + catch-up level scaling).

## Update
Just replace `dinput8.dll` in your Ys Origin folder with the one here, and (for archipelago.gg) set `host=wss://archipelago.gg` in `yso_ap.cfg`. The `.apworld` is unchanged from 1.3.0 apart from the version stamp.
v1.3.1.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.3.1 — Direct archipelago.gg (wss://)

v1.4.0.description:
## Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.4 — Start Anywhere (Random Spawn)

Turn on **`random_start`** (with `statue_warp_locks`) and New Game drops you at a **random goddess statue anywhere in the tower** — playable, ready to go.

### Start anywhere
- **Whole intro skipped, every character.** The opening movies *and* in-engine cutscenes (including Toal's separate intro + arena tutorial) are gone — you warp straight to your spawn statue. No renamed game files: the mod just makes the engine skip the movies.
- **Geared for where you land.** You spawn with a floor-appropriate **level** and **weapon**, and the Crystal / Dark Crystal (by character), so combat is playable wherever you start — not 1-damage. Stays correct through death/respawn.
- **Beatable from any spawn.** Reachability now uses a **bidirectional warp-network logic** (Phase B): the warp hub + two-way room edges + one-way drops, so the seed is solvable no matter where you begin. Normal (non-random) seeds are **byte-identical** to 1.3.x — zero regression.

### Also
- Comprehensive catch-up level coverage (no under-leveled rooms when you roam).
- Cleaner overlay: one line per item, shown as `<item> -> <owner>`.

### Install (same as before)
1. `ys_origin.apworld` → Archipelago's `custom_worlds/`
2. `dinput8.dll` → your Ys Origin game folder
3. `Ys-Origin.yaml` → edit and drop in `Players/`; set `statue_warp_locks: true` + `random_start: true` to start anywhere.

Normal seeds are unaffected — `random_start` is opt-in.
v1.4.0.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.4 — Start Anywhere (Random Spawn)

v1.5.0.description:
## In-game connect menu — no more editing config files

Connect to your Archipelago server right from the **title screen**:

- A native **Archipelago** entry now appears above *New Game* (drawn in the game's own font).
- **Press `F8`** at the title to open a connect form: **Server / Port / Slot / Password**.
- **Tab** or **↑/↓** move between fields, type to edit, **Enter** (or **F5**) connects, **Esc** closes.
- `wss://` is implied (secure by default) and the server defaults to `archipelago.gg`.
- While the form is open the game's own menu input is frozen, so nothing happens behind it.

Editing `yso_ap.cfg` is now **optional** — those values just pre-fill the menu. Set `autoconnect=1` there to connect at startup and skip the menu.

### Assets
- **`dinput8.dll`** — the mod. Drop it in your Ys Origin folder (next to `yso_win.exe`).
- **`ys_origin.apworld`** — world v1.5.0 (unchanged logic; version bump to match the release).
- **`Ys-Origin.yaml`** — example player options.

_Note: native "press Up on New Game" selection is deferred — F8 is the current opener._
v1.5.0.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.5.0 — In-Game Connect Menu

v1.7.0.description:
Big playability + polish release. Highlights since 1.5.0:

**New**
- **Warp-map check tracker** — the in-game Tower warp map now shows `found/total` checks on each floor bar (gold when a floor is fully cleared) plus a bottom-left list for statue-less floors. Positions/colors tunable in `yso_ap.cfg` (`warpmap_*`).
- **Weighted blessing-shop costs** — the F5 overlay shop prices each slot by the tower depth of the item placed there: filler/early upgrades cheap, late medallions/keys/deep warps expensive.

**Fixes**
- **EXP multiplier actually works now** — it had been splicing award code-paths the game doesn't use for normal kills; enemy EXP goes through a third add-path that's now hooked. Default boost is **20x**, flat always-on.
- **Random-start warp order** — statue warp indices now use the game's authored registry order (not a numeric sort), so a random start lands on the correct statue instead of the wrong one / looping.
- **1F random-start** now skips the intro instead of playing it in full; reconnecting as a different character no longer leaks weapon state.
- **Real floor data** — statue floors extracted from the game itself (were off by up to 3 floors), improving level pacing and shop unlocks.

**Gentler defaults**
- `max_starting_floor` 10 → 5, `level_scaling` → `exp_multiplier` (no instant level bump), starter weapon by default (earn it via Cleria Ore), flat always-on EXP.

**UI**
- F5 shop lines reformatted to `<cost> SP => <who>'s <item>` with exact "visit N more floors" on locked slots and larger text; the old F7 tracker panel removed (superseded by the warp map); chat starts collapsed with a `[F6] to open AP chat` hint.

**Install:** drop `dinput8.dll` next to `yso_win.exe`, put `ys_origin.apworld` in your Archipelago `worlds/` (or install it), and use `Ys-Origin.yaml` as your player template.
v1.7.0.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.7 — Warp-Map Tracker, Weighted Shop & Fixes

v1.8.0.description:
Built for — and played end-to-end in — a live multiworld. Two real bugs surfaced during that run and are fixed here.

## ✨ Item toasts
The old "last 5 items" feed is gone. Items now arrive as animated slide-in banners, coloured by their Archipelago tier — **progression** gold (with a gentle pulse), **useful** blue, **filler** white, **trap** red — with a `from Player` / `→ Player` subtitle. Bursts collapse into a single summary instead of spamming.

## 🎲 Traps
`trap_count` (default **6**) turns filler slots into traps, shuffled through the multiworld — you'll send them to others and receive them yourself:

| Trap | Effect |
|---|---|
| **EXP Leech** | Costs you a full level |
| **Chaos Warp** | Yanks you to a random unlocked statue |
| **Butterfingers** | Weapon drops to Lv1 for a few seconds |
| **Blinding Fog** | Screen hazes over for 30s |

## 🐛 Fixes found by actually playing it
- **Granted gear no longer vanishes on save/load.** The game rebuilds its flag block every time a save loads — and with DeathLink on, deaths reload constantly — silently un-owning anything granted since your last save. Confirmed live: 8+ key items (boots, medallions, crest, keys) read as unowned after a single death. Progression/useful grants are now watermarked and re-asserted every tick. Consumables stay spendable.
- **Goal detection works out of the box and can't false-fire.** The ending scene no longer needs a manual capture — it's `S_7002`, verified on a real clear. Because the intro shares that scene range, the goal only reports once you've actually played, so a freshly-started slot can't complete itself.

## 🧹 Item curation
- **Removed three items that never existed.** Ventus / Ignis / Terra Bracelet were invented duplicates sitting on the *same* detect flags as the real artifacts, double-firing their checks. Every character collects the same sacred artifacts (confirmed against the guide). Wind now gates on Cerulean Flabellum. 296 → 293 locations.
- **Warp items are readable**: `18F Warp` instead of `Statue Warp: Layer 5 Save (S_5000)`. Rado's Annex keeps its own name since it shares 18F.

## 🔧 Also
- Warp-map check overlay hides properly after a warp; buying a blessing no longer double-toasts.
- Gentler defaults: random start capped to **5F**, starter weapon, and a flat always-on **20× EXP** multiplier (no more instant level bumps).

---
**Install:** drop `ys_origin.apworld` into Archipelago's `custom_worlds/`, put `dinput8.dll` next to `yso_win.exe`, and use `Ys-Origin.yaml` as your player file.

⚠️ Seeds from earlier versions are **not compatible** (the item pool changed).
v1.8.0.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.8 — Item Toasts, Traps & Multiworld Fixes

v1.8.1.description:
Follow-up to [1.8.0](https://github.com/4rtefakt/ys-origin-archipelago/releases/tag/v1.8.0). **Recommended for anyone on 1.8.0.**

## 🔥 Artifacts now unlock their skill

The three sacred artifacts didn't grant the power that actually lets you *cast*. That power lives in separate cells the vanilla chest sets alongside the artifact — and since those cells can't be their own checks (one chest would fire two, which is why they left the item pool in 1.8.0), **nothing was lighting them up**. You'd receive Crimson Lotusblade or Levinstrike Warhammer and own an item you couldn't use.

Caught on a full playthrough: both artifacts owned, neither skill castable — while a suppressed vanilla chest left the wind power *on* with no Flabellum to show for it.

Receiving an artifact now grants its power too:

| Artifact | Unlocks |
|---|---|
| Cerulean Flabellum | Wind |
| Levinstrike Warhammer | Thunder |
| Crimson Lotusblade | Fire |

Both cells are watermarked, so a save/load or a DeathLink death can't strip a skill either.

---
**Install:** drop `ys_origin.apworld` into Archipelago's `custom_worlds/`, put `dinput8.dll` next to `yso_win.exe`, and use `Ys-Origin.yaml` as your player file.

Seeds from 1.8.0 remain compatible — the fix is in how items are granted, not in the item pool.
v1.8.1.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.8.1 — Artifacts Unlock Their Skills

v1.9.0.description:
> ⚠️ **This release requires Archipelago 0.6.8 or newer.** Previous versions supported 0.6.0+. If you're on an older Archipelago, stay on [1.8.1](https://github.com/4rtefakt/ys-origin-archipelago/releases/tag/v1.8.1).

## 🧠 Logic migrated to Rule Builder

Archipelago recently added [Rule Builder](https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/blob/main/docs/rule%20builder.md), a new way for worlds to express their logic. This release moves the whole ruleset over to it.

**Nothing changes for players** — the logic is identical, seeds generate the same way. This is about correctness insurance.

The reason it matters: our warp-skip edges are entrance rules that test whether a region is reachable. That's the exact case Archipelago requires `register_indirect_condition` for — miss it and the generator won't re-check the warp when the anchor floor becomes reachable, which fails *intermittently* rather than loudly. We were already registering them by hand, correctly. Rule Builder now does it automatically, so that whole class of bug can't creep back in.

Verified on a real generated world: **17 indirect conditions registered across 16 anchor regions**, each correctly mapping an anchor floor to its warp entrance.

Also included: the goal condition and all item/skill gates now go through Rule Builder, and `gui_enabled` follows Archipelago's module move to `Utils`.

**Validated:** 36/36 tests, plus 13 seeds generated beatable across *both* rule paths (normal and random-start/warp-network) — because a missing indirect condition fails intermittently, one seed proves nothing.

## 🤖 AI disclosure

The README now carries an [AI usage disclosure](https://github.com/4rtefakt/ys-origin-archipelago#-ai-usage-disclosure), per Archipelago's request that developers be upfront about it. Short version: AI was used heavily as a pair-programmer, every line was proofread by the maintainer, and nothing ships unplayed — this world has been taken end-to-end through a full multiworld to the credits.

---
**Install:** drop `ys_origin.apworld` into Archipelago's `custom_worlds/`, put `dinput8.dll` next to `yso_win.exe`, and use `Ys-Origin.yaml` as your player file.
v1.9.0.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.9 — Rule Builder (now requires AP 0.6.8)

v1.9.1.description:
> 🚨 **If you downloaded 1.9.0, replace it — it was uninstallable.** It declared a minimum of Archipelago **0.6.8**, which isn't a released version (0.6.7 is current). My mistake: I checked against a source checkout of `main`, which reports the *next* version number before it ships. This release requires **Archipelago 0.6.7 or greater**, verified against the actual 0.6.7 release.

## What's fixed

- **`minimum_ap_version` is now 0.6.7** — the version that actually exists. Confirmed against the 0.6.7 release tag directly: `rule_builder/` is present and `AutoWorld` has `set_rule` / `set_completion_rule` / `_register_rule_indirects`, so Rule Builder is fully supported there.
- **Removed a latent unbeatable-seed bug that the version mistake was hiding.** The rules relied on an empty `And()` resolving to `True_()` — but that change landed in June 2026, *after* 0.6.7 shipped. On 0.6.7 an empty `And()` is still `False_()`, which would have turned a "no requirements" edge impassable and quietly made seeds unbeatable. Nothing in the current data can produce an empty `And()`, so it was latent rather than live — but it could never have been caught locally, since a `main` checkout already has the new behaviour. The rules no longer depend on that behaviour at all.

Everything else is unchanged from [1.9.0](https://github.com/4rtefakt/ys-origin-archipelago/releases/tag/v1.9.0).

**Validated:** 36/36 offline tests, Archipelago's own `test/general` suite, and all 3 characters generating beatable across both rule paths.

---
**Install:** drop `ys_origin.apworld` into Archipelago's `custom_worlds/`, put `dinput8.dll` next to `yso_win.exe`, and use `Ys-Origin.yaml` as your player file.
v1.9.1.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.9.1 — Fixes 1.9.0's impossible AP requirement

v1.9.2.description:
> Requires **Archipelago 0.6.7 or greater**. This ships both an APWorld and a mod `.dll` — **update both**.

## 🏁 Goal reporting for Yunica and Hugo

Only **Toal's** ending was ever confirmed. Yunica and Hugo finish on **Dalles** instead, and their ending scene has never been captured — meaning their slot might never report a goal, which leaves everyone else in the multiworld waiting on their items. That's the worst kind of bug to leave lying around, so:

- **`goal_scene=` now takes a list** (e.g. `goal_scene=7002,7010`), so covering another route is a one-line config change instead of waiting for a release.
- **An unknown ending now tells you what it is.** If you finish on a route we haven't captured, the overlay shows the exact scene number to put in `goal_scene=` — and reports it in the log. The first Yunica or Hugo clear settles it permanently.

If your goal doesn't fire, you're not stuck: ask your host to release the slot, then drop the number the overlay gave you into `yso_ap.cfg` (and please pass it along so it can become the default).

**What this deliberately does *not* do:** auto-fire on any `7xxx` scene after the last boss. That range isn't ending-only — a real Toal endgame runs `7000 → 7080 (Dalles) → 7000 → 7099 (Darm) → 7000 → 0 → 7002`, so `7000` is a hub you walk through *long before winning*. Guessing there risks a **false** goal, which completes your slot and dumps your items mid-run. A missed goal costs a manual release; a false one wrecks the multiworld. Not worth it on a single observed route.

Also: the config parser fails safe (bad value → the goal simply never fires rather than mis-firing) and now logs what it parsed.

---
**Install:** drop `ys_origin.apworld` into Archipelago's `custom_worlds/`, put `dinput8.dll` next to `yso_win.exe`, and use `Ys-Origin.yaml` as your player file.
v1.9.2.title: Ys Origin × Archipelago 1.9.2 — Goal reporting for every route
